description_long: This is a classic wooden barrel with a slightly bulging cylindrical shape, constructed from vertical wooden staves bound together by three wide, dark metallic hoops. The wood has a natural, medium-brown finish with visible grain texture, giving it a rustic and sturdy appearance. The top and bottom surfaces are flat and made of the same wood, tightly fitted within the metal bands. The metallic hoops are slightly reflective and feature large, round rivets for reinforcement, adding to the barrel's robust look. There are no visible handles, spouts, or markings on the exterior, and the surface shows a subtle sheen, suggesting a protective finish. This type of barrel is commonly found in urban settings such as alleys, storage areas, or near taverns and markets.
description: A medium-brown wooden barrel with three dark metal hoops and a rustic, sturdy appearance. The surface is subtly glossy with visible wood grain and no handles or markings.
description_view_0: The barrel's front shows the wood grain and three prominent metal hoops with visible rivets; the top is fully visible.
description_view_1: This side view highlights the curvature of the barrel and the metallic sheen of the hoops, with rivets more pronounced on the left.
description_view_2: The barrel appears symmetrical, with the metal hoops and wood grain evenly distributed; the rivets are less prominent.
description_view_3: This view shows a slight highlight on the upper hoop and a more shadowed wood texture, with no additional features visible.
